replies.author: @kitsune33005
replies.text: You sound like you are unfamiliar with the extensive history of European colonization of Africa, the human rights abuses, the extraction of resources, and the outright racism. Please learn about the relationship between Belgium and the Congo. You could also pay attention to France's current attempts to destabilize the governments of the Sahel by bankrolling terrorists in order to essentially recolonize them. A prior French president admitted that without Africa (meaning its resources), France was nothing. There's simply no excuse for blaming the current conditions of African countries in such a facile manner on the people or their governments without expressing any understanding of the relationship between Western imperialism on those countries. The information is available.

comments.author: @Aventurex-686
comments.text: The older I get, the more I enjoy documentaries that replace assumptions with curiosity. Congo feels like a giant treasure chest where the greatest discoveries are the people and everyday life, not just the scenery. Which moment surprised you the most? I'd happily watch a whole series like this.

replies.author: @BestTravel2
replies.text: That's beautifully put! I completely agree that the people and their everyday lives are the true treasures. For me, the moment that surprised me the most was definitely seeing the incredible resourcefulness in how they adapted to their environment. A whole series would be amazing to explore even more!
